author: Prakash Dev, Shriddheya
title: Maharshi Narada ji ka Sankshipt Jiwan Charitr
gloss: Or a short biography of the Hindu sage Narada who was the author of the Bhakti Sutras or aphorism on the love of God, some quotations from which are given in the book.

author: Shiv Brit Lal, M.A., Babu
title: Chittaur ka Shaka
gloss: Or the third part of the Bharat ki Shuja aur Alim Striyon ke Karname. This is a biography of the queen of Chittaur, who fought against Akbar the Great for a long time.
notes: Part III of the Bharat ki Shuja aur Alim Striyon ke Karname series (verso index: 'p.4.2 Bharat ke Shya II (H.)') — Rajput queen of Chittor vs Akbar; Hindu heroic-womanhood history.

author: Ali Bakhsh, Raja
title: Suwang Nal Daman
gloss: A dramatic adaptation of the Mahabharta episode of Nala and Damyanti.
copyright: Hakim Sayad Maqbul Husain, Rewari; No. 28, dated the 24th February, 1910.
notes: Muslim author/publisher (Ali Bakhsh / Hakim Maqbul Husain) staging the Mahabharata's Nala-Damayanti as a swang — cross-tradition popular theatre in Hindi-in-Urdu-script.

author: Ali Bakhsh, Raja
title: Suwang Fasana-i-Ajaib
gloss: A dramatic adaptation of the story of Fasana-i-Ajaib of Sarur, of Lucknow.
copyright: Hakim Sayad Maqbul Husain, Rewari; No. 29, dated the 24th February, 1910.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s1; Rajab Ali Beg Surur's Fasana-i-Ajaib dramatized.

author: Ali Bakhsh, Raja
title: Suang Padmawat
gloss: A drama in which a Hindu princess learns from an astrologer that she would be married to a certain prince with whom she opens negotiations and is ultimately married.
publisher: Hakim Sayad Maqbul Husain, Proprietor, Maqbul-i-'Am Press
copyright: Hakim Sayad Maqbul Husain, Rewári; No. 40, dated the 24th February, 1910.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s2; the Padmavat legend as swang. Three swangs registered together (regs 288, 289, 381; copyrights Nos. 28, 29, 40).

author: Asha Ram, Lala
title: Hindi Lipi Shiksha, Pratham Bhag
gloss: Or Hindi Copy Slip, Part I.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Hindi copybook Part I, 5,000 copies.

author: Asha Ram, Lala
title: Varn Shikshak, Pratham Ank
gloss: Or a Hindi Primer, Part I.

author: Asha Ram, Lala
title: Varn Shikshak, Dutiya Ank
notes: Author dash-continuation of s2; primer Part II.
